title: Exception frontmatter
_comment: Schema for exception primitives. An exception is a documented deviation from a workflow, fact, or rule, with the conditions under which the deviation applies. Downstream consumers (workflow consumers, audit reviewers, future exception-rebuild aggregator) depend on these fields. Malformed YAML hides the exception from automation, which can re-cause the underlying defect. Includes the cross-type frontmatter contract (provenance, confidence, freshness_days, last_verified, source_count, memory_class, entity_ids) shared across all 8 typed memory primitives. memory_class default for exception is procedural. entity_ids cross-source linking (slack, github_pr, github_issue, jira, notion, gmail, linear).

rule_id.description: Pointer to the workflow, fact, or rule the exception deviates from. Free-form id or wikilink target.

exception_summary.description: One-sentence description of the exception and when it applies.

frequency_observed.description: Count of times this exception has been observed.

owner.description: Person or role who carries the exception.

approved_by.description: Person or role who approved the deviation.

memory_class.description: Episodic captures one-time events; procedural captures repeatable, time-stable knowledge. Default for exception is procedural.

entity_ids.description: Cross-source identifiers. Keys: slack, github_pr, github_issue, jira, notion, gmail, linear. Values: source-system IDs.
